title.en: GUIDING RAILWAY TRAFFIC; ENSURING THE SAFETY OF RAILWAY TRAFFIC (brakes or auxiliary equipment B61H, B61K; point or crossing construction E01B)

Devices along the route controlled by interaction with the vehicle or train (detonators B61L5/20; operation of points or signals by passage of the vehicle B61L11/00, B61L13/00; operation of gates, or gates and signals, by approaching vehicle B61L29/18)
Electric devices associated with track {, e.g. rail contacts}
Electric devices associated with overhead trolley wires
Devices for indicating the passing of the end of the vehicle or train
Devices for counting axles; Devices for counting vehicles
Railway track circuits (rail joints E01B11/00, e.g. insulated rail joints E01B11/54)
Safety arrangements for preventing or indicating malfunction of the device, e.g. by leakage current, by lightning
Devices along the route for controlling devices on the vehicle or train, e.g. to release brake or to operate a warning signal
at selected places along the route, e.g. intermittent control {simultaneous mechanical and electrical control}
Continuous control along the route
Local operating mechanisms for points or track-mounted scotch-blocks; Visible or audible signals; Local operating mechanisms for visible or audible signals (B61L11/00 takes precedence)
Mechanical devices for operating points or scotch-blocks {, e.g. local manual control}
Fluid-pressure devices for operating points or scotch-blocks
Electric devices for operating points or scotch-blocks {, e.g. using electromotive driving means}
Underground actuating arrangements, e.g. for tramways
Locking mechanisms for points; Means for indicating the setting of points
Visible signals
Audible signals, e.g. detonator {audible signalling}
Remote control of local operating means for points, signals, or track-mounted scotch-blocks (B61L11/00, B61L13/00 take precedence; interlocking arrangements B61L19/00)
using mechanical transmission, e.g. wire, lever
using fluid-pressure transmission
using electrical transmission
Illumination specially adapted for points, form signals, or gates
non-electric
electric
Operation of points from the vehicle or by the passage of the vehicle
using mechanical interaction between vehicle and track
using electrical or magnetic interaction between vehicle and track
Operation of signals from the vehicle or by the passage of the vehicle
{actuated by the passage of the vehicle}
{optically actuated}
{acoustically actuated}
using mechanical interaction between vehicle and track
using electrical or magnetic interaction between vehicle and track {, e.g. by conductor circuits using special means or special conductors}
Indicators provided on the vehicle or train for signalling purposes
{wiring diagrams for start- or stop-signals on vehicles having one or more carriages and having electrical communication lines between the carriages}
{Communication with or on the vehicle or train}
{Destination indicators, identification panels or distinguishing signs on the vehicles}
{Train integrity supervision, e.g. end-of-train [EOT] devices}
{On-board optimisation of vehicle or vehicle train operation}
{On-board target speed calculation or supervision}
{Multiple on-board control systems, e.g. "2 out of 3"-systems}
{On-board train data handling}
{On-board diagnosis or maintenance}
{On-board display devices}
{Memory means reproducing during the running of the vehicle or vehicle train, e.g. smart cards}
{Recorders on the vehicle}
Head or tail indicators, e.g. light
Switching systems for classification yards
Details, e.g. indicating degree of track filling
Arrangements for interlocking between points and signals by means of a single interlocking device {, e.g. central control}
Interlocking devices having mechanical or fluid-pressure operation
Interlocking devices having electrical operation
Station blocking between signal boxes in one yard
Mechanical locking and release of the route; Repeat locks; Coupling of semaphores
Electrical locking and release of the route; Electrical repeat locks
Vehicle-on-line indication; Monitoring locking and release of the route
Order transmission and reception arrangements for giving or withholding permission
Arrangements for trains which are closely following one another
Control, warning or like safety means along the route or between vehicles or trains
{Control or safety means for heart-points and crossings of aerial railways, funicular rack-railway}
{Safety arrangements on railway crossings}
for indicating along the route the failure of brakes
for monitoring the mechanical state of the route
for warning men working on the route
for controlling traffic in one direction only
for controlling traffic in two directions over the same pair of rails
for indicating the distance between vehicles or trains by the transmission of signals therebetween
Recording or indicating positions or identities of vehicles or trains or setting of track apparatus
Indicating or recording positions or identities of vehicles or trains
Indicating or recording the setting of track apparatus, e.g. of points, of signals
Central railway traffic control systems; Trackside control; Communication systems specially adapted therefor
Manual systems
Automatic systems, e.g. controlled by train; Change-over to manual control
Operations, e.g. scheduling or time tables
Trackside control of safe travel of vehicle or train, e.g. braking curve calculation
Trackside multiple control systems, e.g. switch-over between different systems
Handling position reports or trackside vehicle data
Trackside diagnosis or maintenance, e.g. software upgrades
Testing or simulation
Details of trackside communication
Safety means for rail/road crossing traffic
Guards or obstacles for preventing access to the route (cattle guards connected to the permanent way E01B17/00)
Gates for level crossings
Operation of gates; Combined operation of gates and signals
Means for warning road traffic that a gate is closed or closing, or that rail traffic is approaching, e.g. for visible or audible warning
Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass
{Autonomous vehicles, i.e. under distributed traffic control}
Control methods
Fuzzy control
Communication or navigation systems for railway traffic
Global system for mobile communication - railways [GSM-R]
Satellite based navigation systems, e.g. global positioning system [GPS]
Features of light signals
using light-emitting diodes [LEDs]
Vehicle systems
Single autonomous vehicles
Magnetic elevation vehicles [maglev]
